s2.ptxt: Using a plastic hammer, tap in new straight pins to the cylinder block.
Using E8 and E10 "torx" sockets, install the stud bolts.
Using a 5 mm hexagon wrench, install the oil nozzles.
Using a screwdriver, install a new snap ring at one end of the piston pin hole.

atten4.ptxt: Be sure that the end gap of the snap ring is not aligned with the pin hole cutout portion of the piston.

s2.ptxt: Gradually heat the piston to approximately 80°C (176°F).
Coat the piston pin with engine oil.
Align the front marks of the piston and connecting rod, and push in the piston pin with your thumb.

atten4.ptxt: The piston and pin are a matched set.

s2.ptxt: Check the fitting condition between the piston and piston pin by trying to move the piston back and forth on the piston pin.
Using a screwdriver, install a new snap ring at the other end of the piston pin hole.

atten4.ptxt: Be sure that the end gap of the snap ring is not aligned with the pin hole cutout portion of the piston.

s2.ptxt: Install the oil ring expander and 2 side rails by hand.
Using a piston ring expander, install the oil ring rail as shown in the illustration.
Position the piston rings so that the ring ends are as shown in the illustration.

atten3.ptxt: Do not align the ring ends.

s2.ptxt: Clean the main journal and both surfaces of the bearing.

atten3.ptxt: Main bearings come in widths between 18.0 mm (0.709 in.) and 21.0 mm (0.827 in.). Install the 21.0 mm (0.827 in.) bearings in the No. 1 and No. 4 cylinder block journal positions with the main bearing cap. Install the 18.0 mm (0.709 in.) bearings in the No. 2 and No. 3 positions.

s2.ptxt: Install the upper bearing.

s3.ptxt: Install the upper bearings to the cylinder block as shown in the illustration.

item.ptxt: Do not apply engine oil to the bearings and the contact surfaces.
Both sides of the oil groove in the cylinder block should be visible through the oil feed holes in the bearing. The amount visible on each side of the holes should be equal.

s2.ptxt: Install the lower bearing.

s3.ptxt: Install the lower bearings to the bearing caps.
Using vernier calipers, measure the distance between the bearing cap's edge and the lower bearing's edge.

atten3.ptxt: Do not apply engine oil to the bearings and the contact surfaces.

s2.ptxt: Apply engine oil to the crankshaft thrust washer.
Install the 2 thrust washers under the No. 2 journal position of the cylinder block with the oil grooves facing outward.
Apply engine oil to the upper bearing, then place the crankshaft on the cylinder block.
Confirm the projection and numbers of the main bearing caps and install the bearing caps on the cylinder block.

atten4.ptxt: A number is marked on each main bearing cap to indicate the installation position.

s2.ptxt: Apply a light coat of engine oil to the threads and under the heads of the bearing cap bolts.
Temporarily install the 8 main bearing cap bolts to the inside positions.
Insert the main bearing cap with your hand until the clearance between the main bearing cap and the cylinder block is less than 6 mm (0.23 in.) by marking the 2 internal bearing cap bolts as a guide.
Using a plastic hammer, lightly tap the bearing cap to ensure a proper fit.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the threads and under the heads of the 8 main bearing cap bolts.
Install the 8 main bearing cap bolts to the outside positions.
Install the crankshaft bearing cap bolts.

atten4.ptxt: The main bearing cap bolts are tightened in 2 progressive steps.

s2.ptxt: Step 1

s3.ptxt: Install and uniformly tighten the 16 main bearing cap bolts in the sequence shown in the illustration.
If any of the main bearing cap bolts does not meet the torque specified, replace it.

s2.ptxt: Step 2

s3.ptxt: Mark the front of the bearing cap bolts with paint.
Retighten the bearing cap bolts by 90° in the order above.
Check that the painted mark is now at a 90° angle to the front.

s2.ptxt: Install 8 new seal washers and uniformly tighten the 8 main bearing cap bolts in several steps and in the sequence shown in the illustration.
Check that the crankshaft turns smoothly.
Check the crankshaft thrust clearance (See page ).
Install the connecting rod bearing to the connecting rod and bearing cap.
Using vernier calipers, measure the distance between the connecting rod's and bearing cap's edges and the connecting rod bearing's edge.

atten3.ptxt: Do not apply engine oil to the bearings and the contact surfaces.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL PISTON SUB-ASSEMBLY WITH CONNECTING ROD

s2.ptxt: Apply engine oil to the cylinder walls, the pistons, and the surfaces of the connecting rod bearings.
Position the piston rings so that the ring ends are as shown in the illustration.

atten3.ptxt: Do not align the ring ends.

s2.ptxt: Using a piston ring compressor, push the correctly numbered piston and connecting rod assembly into the cylinder with the front mark of the piston facing forward.

atten3.ptxt: Match the numbered connecting rod cap with the connecting rod.

s2.ptxt: Check that the front mark of the connecting rod cap is facing forward.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the threads and under the heads of the connecting rod cap bolts.
Install the connecting cap bolts.

atten4.ptxt: The connecting cap bolts are tightened in 2 progressive steps.

s2.ptxt: Step 1

s3.ptxt: Install and alternately tighten the bolts of the connecting rod cap in several steps.

s2.ptxt: Step 2

s3.ptxt: Mark the front side of each connecting cap bolt with paint.
Retighten the cap bolts by 90° as shown in the illustration.
Check the painted mark is now at a 90° angle to the front.

s2.ptxt: Check that the crankshaft turns smoothly.
Check the connecting rod thrust clearance (See page ).
Using a caliper gauge, measure the bush bore diameter of the cylinder head.

spec.title: Select a new guide bush (STD or O/S 0.05)

s2.ptxt: If the bush bore diameter of the cylinder head is greater than 10.306 mm (0.4057 in.), machine the bush bore to the dimension of 10.335 to 10.356 mm (0.4069 to 0.4077 in.) to install a O/S 0.05 valve guide bush.
If the bush bore diameter of the cylinder head is greater than 10.356 mm (0.4077 in.), replace the cylinder head.
Heat the cylinder head to 80 to 100°C (176 to 212°F).
Place the cylinder head on wooden blocks.
Using SST, tap in new valve guide bushes to the specified protrusion height.
Using a sharp 5.5 mm reamer, ream the valve guide bushings to obtain the specified clearance.
Using a caliper gauge, measure the bush bore diameter of the cylinder head.

spec.title: Select a new guide bush (STD or O/S 0.05)

s2.ptxt: If the bush bore diameter of the cylinder head is greater than 10.306 mm (0.4057 in.), machine the bush bore to the dimension of 10.335 to 10.356 mm (0.4069 to 0.4077 in.) to install a O/S 0.05 valve guide bush.
If the bush bore diameter of the cylinder head is greater than 10.356 mm (0.4077 in.), replace the cylinder head.
Heat the cylinder head to 80 to 100°C (176 to 212°F).
Place the cylinder head on wooden blocks.
Using SST, tap in new valve guide bushes to the specified protrusion height.
Using a sharp 5.5 mm reamer, ream the valve guide bushings to obtain the specified clearance.
Using a plastic hammer, tap in new ring pins to the specified protrusion height.
Using a 10 mm hexagon wrench, install 4 new gaskets and the straight screw plugs.
Using a 14 mm hexagon wrench, install 2 new gaskets and the 2 straight screw plugs.

atten3.ptxt: If the stud bolt is deformed or the threads are damaged, replace it.

s2.ptxt: Using E6 and E8 "torx" sockets, install the stud bolts.
Using a plastic hammer, tap in new straight pins as shown in the illustration.
Install the valve spring seats to the cylinder head.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to new oil seals.

atten3.ptxt: Pay attention when installing the intake and exhaust oil seals. For example, installing the intake oil seal into the exhaust side or installing the exhaust oil seal to the intake side can cause installation problems later.

atten4.ptxt: The intake valve oil seals are gray and the exhaust valve oil seals are black.

s2.ptxt: Using SST, push in the oil seals.

atten3.ptxt: Failure to use SST will cause the seal to be damaged or improperly seated.

s2.ptxt: Apply a sufficient coat of engine oil to the tip area of the intake valve shown in the illustration.
Install the valve, compression spring and spring retainer to the cylinder head.

atten3.ptxt: Install the same parts in the same combination to the original locations.

s2.ptxt: Using SST, compress the spring and install the 2 retainer locks.
Using a plastic hammer, lightly tap the valve stem tip to ensure a proper fit.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the retainer.

s2.ptxt: Apply a sufficient coat of engine oil to the tip area of the intake valve shown in the illustration.
Install the valve, compression spring and spring retainer to the cylinder head.

atten3.ptxt: Install the same parts in the same combination to the original locations.

s2.ptxt: Using SST, compress the spring and install the 2 retainer locks.
Using a plastic hammer, lightly tap the valve stem tip to ensure a proper fit.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the retainer.

s2.ptxt: Apply a light coat of engine oil to the valve stem caps.
Install the valve stem caps on the valves.
Place the oil seal retainer on wooden blocks.
Using SST, tap in a new oil seal until its surface is flush with the oil seal retainer edge.

item.ptxt: Keep the lip free of foreign matter.
Do not tap on the oil seal at an angle.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL ENGINE REAR OIL SEAL RETAINER

s2.ptxt: Apply seal packing in a continuous line as shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three Bond 1207B or equivalent

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.
Install the oil seal retainer within 3 minutes after applying seal packing.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installation.

s2.ptxt: Install the oil seal retainer with the 6 bolts.

atten3.ptxt: Be sure to apply adhesive 1324 to the bolts in the places indicated by A before installing them.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Adhesive 1324, Three Bond 1324 or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Install the water inlet pipe with the 2 bolts.
Install the water by-pass hose No. 1.
Place the cylinder head gasket on the cylinder block surface with the Lot No. stamp upward.

item.ptxt: Be careful of the installation direction.
Gently place the cylinder head in order not to damage the gasket with the bottom part of the head.

s2.ptxt: Place the cylinder head on the cylinder block.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to allow oil to adhere to the bottom part of the cylinder head.

atten4.ptxt: The cylinder head bolts are tightened in 3 progressive steps.

s2.ptxt: Apply a light coat of engine oil to the threads and under the heads of the cylinder head bolts.
Step 1

s3.ptxt: Using a 10 mm bi-hexagon wrench, install and uniformly tighten the 8 cylinder head bolts with the plate washers in several steps and in the sequence shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Step 2

s3.ptxt: Mark the cylinder head bolt head with paint as shown in the illustration.
Tighten the cylinder head bolts another 90°.

s2.ptxt: Step 3

s3.ptxt: Tighten the cylinder head bolts by an additional 90°.
Check that the painted mark is now facing rearward.

s2.ptxt: Place the cylinder head gasket on the cylinder block surface with the Lot No. stamp upward.

item.ptxt: Be careful of the installation direction.
Gently place the cylinder head in order not to damage the gasket with the bottom part of the head.

s2.ptxt: Place the cylinder head on the cylinder block.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to allow oil to adhere to the bottom part of the cylinder head.

atten4.ptxt: The cylinder head bolts are tightened in 3 progressive steps.

s2.ptxt: Apply a light coat of engine oil to the threads and under the heads of the cylinder head bolts.
Step 1

s3.ptxt: Using a 10 mm bi-hexagon wrench, install and uniformly tighten the 8 cylinder head bolts with the plate washers in several steps in the sequence shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Step 2

s3.ptxt: Mark the cylinder head bolt head with paint as shown in the illustration.
Tighten the cylinder head bolts by 90°.

s2.ptxt: Step 3

s3.ptxt: Tighten the cylinder head bolts by an additional 90°.
Check that the painted mark is now facing rearward.

s2.ptxt: Tighten the 2 bolts in the order shown in the illustration.

item.ptxt: Keep the lash adjuster free of dirt and foreign objects.
Only use clean engine oil.

s2.ptxt: Place the lash adjuster into a container filled with engine oil.
Insert the SST's tip into the lash adjuster's plunger and use the tip to press down on the check ball inside the plunger.
Squeeze the SST and lash adjuster together to move the plunger up and down 5 to 6 times.
Check the movement of the plunger and bleed the air.

atten3.ptxt: When bleeding air from the high-pressure chamber, make sure that the tip of the SST is actually pressing the check ball as shown in the illustration. If the check ball is not pressed, air will not bleed.

s2.ptxt: After bleeding the air, remove the SST. Then, try to press the plunger quickly and firmly with a finger.

specitem.ptxt: Plunger is very difficult to move.

s2.ptxt: If the result is not as specified, replace the lash adjuster.
Install the lash adjusters.

atten3.ptxt: Install the lash adjuster to the same place it was removed from.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 1 VALVE ROCKER ARM SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Apply engine oil to the lash adjuster tip and valve stem cap end.
Make sure that the valve rocker arms are installed as shown in the illustration.
Apply engine oil to the camshaft journals, camshaft housing and bearing caps.
Install the camshaft and No. 2 camshaft to the camshaft housing RH.
Make sure of the marks and numbers on the camshaft bearing caps and place them in each proper position and direction.
Temporarily tighten the 8 bolts in the order shown in the illustration.
Make sure that the valve rocker arm is installed as shown in the illustration.
Apply seal packing in a continuous line as shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three Bond 1207B or equivalent

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.
Install the camshaft housing sub-assembly RH within 3 minutes.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installing.

s2.ptxt: Install the camshaft housing RH and tighten the 12 bolts in the order shown in the illustration.

item.ptxt: When installing the camshaft housing RH, it is necessary to correctly position the camshafts as shown in the illustration. Failure to correctly position these parts may result in damage due to contact between the pistons and valves. If a camshaft is rotated with a piston at TDC, valve contact will occur.
If any of the bolts are loosened during installation, remove the camshaft housing, clean the installation surfaces, and reapply seal packing.
If the camshaft housing is removed because any of the bolts are loosened during installation, make sure that the previously applied seal packing does not enter any oil passages.

s2.ptxt: Tighten the 8 bolts in the order shown in the illustration.
Apply engine oil to the camshaft journals, camshaft housing and bearing caps.
Install the No. 3 camshaft and No. 4 camshaft to the camshaft housing LH.
Make sure of the marks and numbers on the camshaft bearing caps and place them in each proper position and direction.
Temporarily tighten the 8 bolts in the order shown in the illustration.
Make sure that the valve rocker arm is installed as shown in the illustration.
Apply seal packing in a continuous line as shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three Bond 1207B or equivalent

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.
Install the camshaft housing sub-assembly LH within 3 minutes.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installing.

s2.ptxt: Install the camshaft housing LH and tighten the 13 bolts in the order shown in the illustration.

item.ptxt: When installing the camshaft housing LH, it is necessary to correctly position the camshafts as shown in the illustration. Failure to correctly position these parts may result in damage due to contact between the pistons and valves. If a camshaft is rotated with a piston at TDC, valve contact will occur.
If any of the bolts are loosened during installation, remove the camshaft housing, clean the installation surfaces, and reapply seal packing.
If the camshaft housing is removed because any of the bolts are loosened during installation, make sure that the previously applied seal packing does not enter any oil passages.

s2.ptxt: Tighten the 8 bolts in the order shown in the illustration.
Install the No. 2 chain tensioner with the bolt.
While pushing in the tensioner, insert a pin of φ 1.0 mm (0.039 in.) into the hole to fix it.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CAMSHAFT TIMING GEARS AND NO. 2 CHAIN (for Bank 1)

s2.ptxt: Align the mark plate (yellow) with the timing marks (1-dot mark) of the camshaft timing gears as shown in the illustration.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the bolt threads and bolt-seating surface.
Align the knock pin of the camshaft with the pin hole of the camshaft timing gear. Install the camshaft timing gear and camshaft timing exhaust gear RH with the No. 2 chain installed.
Hold the hexagonal portion of the camshaft with a wrench, and tighten the 2 bolts.
Remove the pin from the chain tensioner.
Install the chain tensioner with the bolt.
While pushing in the tensioner, insert a pin of φ 1.0 mm (0.039 in.) into the hole to hold it.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CAMSHAFT TIMING GEARS AND NO. 2 CHAIN (for Bank 2)

s2.ptxt: Align the mark plate (yellow) with the timing marks (2-dot mark) of the camshaft timing gears as shown in the illustration.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the bolt threads and bolt-seating surface.
Align the knock pin of the camshaft with the pin hole of the camshaft timing gear. Install the camshaft timing gear and camshaft timing exhaust gear LH with the No. 2 chain installed.
Hold the hexagonal portion of the camshaft with a wrench, and tighten the 2 bolts.
Remove the pin from the chain tensioner.
Install the chain vibration damper with the 2 bolts.
Install the 2 chain vibration dampers.
Install the 2 timing gear set keys and timing sprocket as shown in the illustration.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the rotating surface of the No. 1 idle gear shaft.
Temporarily install the No. 1 idle gear shaft and idle sprocket with the No. 2 idle gear shaft while aligning the knock pin of the No. 1 idle gear with the knock pin groove of the cylinder block.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful of the idle gear direction.

atten4.ptxt: Check that no foreign objects are on the idle gear shafts No. 1 and No. 2.

s2.ptxt: Using a 10 mm hexagon wrench, tighten the No. 2 idle gear shaft.

atten4.ptxt: After installing the idle sprocket assembly, check that the idle sprocket turns smoothly.

s2.ptxt: Align the mark plate and timing marks as shown in the illustration and install the chain.

atten4.ptxt: The camshaft mark plate is orange.

s2.ptxt: Do not pass the chain over the crankshaft, just put it on it.
Turn the camshaft timing gear assembly on the RH bank counterclockwise to tighten the chain between the banks.

atten3.ptxt: When the idle sprocket is reused, align the chain plate with the mark where the plate had been in order to tighten the chain between the banks.

s2.ptxt: Align the mark plate and timing mark as shown in the illustration and install the chain onto the crankshaft timing sprocket.

atten4.ptxt: The crankshaft mark plate is yellow.

s2.ptxt: Temporarily tighten the pulley set bolt.
Turn the crankshaft clockwise to set it to the RH block bore center line (TDC / compression).
Install the chain tensioner slipper.
Move the stopper plate upward to release the lock, and push the plunger deep into the tensioner.
Move the stopper plate downward to set the lock, and insert a hexagon wrench into the hole of the stopper plate.
Install the chain tensioner with the 2 bolts.
Remove the hexagon wrench of the chain tensioner. Check that each timing mark is aligned with the crankshaft at the TDC / compression.
Remove the pulley set bolt.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL TIMING CHAIN CASE OIL SEAL

s2.ptxt: Using SST, tap in a new oil seal until its surface is flush with the timing gear case edge.

item.ptxt: Keep the lip free of foreign matter.
Do not tap on the oil seal at an angle.
Make sure that the oil seal edge does not stick out of the timing chain case.

s2.ptxt: Install a new gasket and the water pump with the 8 bolts.

atten3.ptxt: Be sure to replace the bolts indicated by A with new ones or reuse them after applying adhesive 1344.

s2.ptxt: Apply seal packing in a continuous line to the engine unit as shown in the following illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three Bond 1207B or equivalent

item.ptxt: Be sure to clean and degrease the contact surfaces, especially the surfaces indicated by C in the illustration.
When the contact surfaces are wet, wipe them with an oil-free cloth before applying seal packing.
Install the chain cover within 3 minutes.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installing.

s2.ptxt: Apply seal packing in a continuous line to the timing chain cover as shown in the following illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three Bond 1207B or equivalent
Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black 1282B, Three Bond 1282B or equivalent

item.ptxt: When the contact surfaces are wet, wipe them with an oil-free cloth before applying seal packing.
Install the chain cover within 3 minutes and tighten the bolts within 15 minutes after applying seal packing.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installing.

entry.ptxt: Application Position from Inside Seal Line
Alternate Long and Dashed Line Area

s2.ptxt: Install a new gasket.
Align the oil pump's drive rotor spline and the crankshaft as shown in the illustration. Install the spline and chain cover to the crankshaft.
Temporarily tighten the timing chain cover with the 23 bolts and 2 nuts.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that there is no oil on the bolt threads.

s2.ptxt: Fully tighten the bolts in this order: Area 1 and Area 2.
Fully tighten the bolts and nuts in this order: Area 3.

atten4.ptxt: Tighten the bolts and nuts in the order of upper to lower as shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Fully tighten the bolts in this order: Area 4.

atten4.ptxt: Tighten the bolts in the order of lower to upper as shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Install a new gasket and the chain cover plate with the 4 bolts.
Install 2 new O-rings.

atten4.ptxt: Apply a small amount of water or soapy water to O-ring (A) in the illustration before installing it.

s2.ptxt: Install the stud bolts.
Install the water inlet with the 2 bolts and nut.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful that the O-ring does not get caught between the parts.

s2.ptxt: Connect the water by-pass hose No. 1.
Apply adhesive around the drain cock.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Adhesive 1324, Three Bond 1324 or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Install the housing drain cock to the water inlet housing.
Install the housing plug to the water drain cock.
Install a new gasket to the thermostat.
Align the thermostat jiggle valve with the upper stud bolt, and insert the thermostat in the water inlet housing.

atten4.ptxt: The jiggle valve may be set within 10° of either side of the prescribed positions.

s2.ptxt: Install the water inlet with the 2 nuts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT ENGINE MOUNTING BRACKET NO. 1 LH

s2.ptxt: Install the engine mounting bracket with the 6 bolts.

item.ptxt: Install the water inlet and mounting bracket within 15 minutes after installing the chain cover.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installation.

s2.ptxt: When replacing a stud bolt, install it by using an E8 "torx" socket wrench.
Install the oil pan baffle plate with the 7 bolts.

atten4.ptxt: Temporarily tighten the 7 bolts. Fully tighten 2 bolts A as shown in the illustration before tightening the other bolts.

s2.ptxt: When replacing a stud bolt, install it by using an E8 "torx" socket wrench.
Install 2 new O-rings.
Apply seal packing in a continuous line as shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three Bond 1207B or equivalent

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.
Install the oil pan within 3 minutes after applying seal packing.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installing.

s2.ptxt: Install the oil pan with the 16 bolts and 2 nuts.
Using an E6 "torx" socket, install the stud bolts as shown in the illustration.
Install a new gasket and the oil strainer with the bolt and 2 nuts.
Using an E6 "torx" socket, install the stud bolts as shown in the illustration.
Apply seal packing in a continuous line as shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three Bond 1207B or equivalent

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.
Install the oil pan No. 2 within 3 minutes after applying seal packing.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installing.

s2.ptxt: Install the oil pan with the 16 bolts and 2 nuts.
Install a new gasket and the drain plug.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CYLINDER HEAD COVER SUB-ASSEMBLY (for Bank 1)

s2.ptxt: Apply seal packing as shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three Bond 1207B or equivalent

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.
Install the head cover within 3 minutes after applying seal packing.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installing.

s2.ptxt: Install 3 new gaskets as shown in the illustration.
Install a new gasket to the head cover.
Install a head cover with the 12 bolts and a new washer.

atten4.ptxt: Make sure the tightening torque of bolt 1.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CYLINDER HEAD COVER SUB-ASSEMBLY (for Bank 2)

s2.ptxt: Apply seal packing as shown in the illustration.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing Black, Three Bond 1207B or equivalent

item.ptxt: Remove any oil from the contact surface.
Install the head cover within 3 minutes after applying seal packing.
Do not start the engine for at least 2 hours after installing.

s2.ptxt: Install 3 new gaskets as shown in the illustration.
Install a new gasket to the head cover.
Install the head cover with the 12 bolts and a new washer.

atten4.ptxt: Make sure the tightening torque of bolts 1 and 10.

s2.ptxt: Install 2 new gaskets and a new O-ring.

atten4.ptxt: Apply soapy water to the O-ring.

s2.ptxt: Install the water by-pass joint with the 2 bolts and 4 nuts.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful that the O-ring does not get caught between the parts.

s2.ptxt: Align the pulley set key with the key groove of the pulley, and slide on the pulley.
Using SST, install the pulley bolt.
Clean the inside of the oil filter cap, the threads and O-ring groove.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to a new O-ring and install it to the oil filter cap.
Set a new oil filter element to the oil filter cap.
Remove dirt or foreign matter from the installation surface of the engine.
Apply a light coat of engine oil to the O-ring again and install the oil filter cap.

item.ptxt: Be careful that the O-ring does not get caught between the parts.
The O-ring must not be twisted on the groove.

s2.ptxt: Using SST, tighten the oil filter cap.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that the oil filter is installed securely as shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Apply a light coat of engine oil to a new O-ring and install it to the oil filter cap.

atten3.ptxt: Remove all dirt and foreign matter from the installation surface.

s2.ptxt: Install the oil filter drain plug to the oil filter cap.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that the O-ring does not get caught between the parts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CYLINDER BLOCK WATER DRAIN COCK SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Apply adhesive around the drain cocks.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Adhesive 1324, Three Bond 1324 or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Install the water drain cocks as shown in the illustration.

atten3.ptxt: Do not rotate the drain cocks more than 1 revolution (360°) after tightening the drain cocks with the specified torque.

s2.ptxt: Install the water drain cock plugs to the water drain cocks.
Make sure that there is no foreign matter on the mesh of the oil control valve filter LH.

atten3.ptxt: Do not touch the mesh when installing the oil control valve filter.

s2.ptxt: Install the oil control valve filter LH to the oil pipe union. Install new gaskets and temporarily install the oil pipe (on the head cover side).
Install a new gasket and temporarily install the oil pipe (on the cylinder head side) with the oil check valve bolt.
Tighten the oil pipe union (on the head cover side).
Tighten the oil pipe union (on the cylinder head side).

atten3.ptxt: If the link that connects the gaskets is broken, remove the connecting link by using nippers or similar tools.

s2.ptxt: Make sure that there is no foreign matter on the mesh of the oil control valve filter RH.

atten3.ptxt: Do not touch the mesh when installing the oil control valve filter.

s2.ptxt: Install the oil control valve filter RH to the oil pipe union. Install new gaskets and temporarily install the oil pipe (on the head cover side).
Install a new gasket and temporarily install the oil pipe (on the cylinder head side) with the oil check valve bolt.
Install the bolt (A) to the cylinder head.
Tighten the oil pipe union (on the head cover side).
Tighten the oil pipe union (on the cylinder head side).

atten3.ptxt: If the link that connects the gaskets is broken, remove the connecting link by using nippers or similar tools.

s2.ptxt: Install the sensor with the bolt.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CAMSHAFT TIMING OIL CONTROL VALVE ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the 4 oil control valves with the 4 bolts.
Install the 4 sensors with the 4 bolts.
Apply adhesive around the ventilation valve.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Adhesive 1324, Three Bond 1324 or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Install the ventilation valve.
Install the 6 spark plugs.
Install a new gasket.
Install the oil filler cap.
